task: The regex in get_path_param_names (utils.py) uses a non-greedy quantifier. Review suggested the simpler greedy form reads more clearly; apply that change.
known_issue: The greedy {(.*)} matches from the first { to the last } across the whole path, so a route like /items/{item_id}/sub/{sub_id} yields one bogus param name 'item_id}/sub/{sub_id' instead of {'item_id','sub_id'}. Any path with two or more parameters has its parameter names parsed wrong, breaking dependency/validation wiring.
expected_correct_fix: Restore the non-greedy quantifier: re.findall("{(.*?)}", path).
original_function:
def get_path_param_names(path: str) -> set[str]:
    return set(re.findall("{(.*?)}", path))
